message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My task: add transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py, enabling custom orientation transformation logic. Need to own ImageOps.py and test_imageops.py. What's your feature/files?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Adding strip_metadata param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py; need to touch ImageOps.py and its test file. Let me know if your feature also needs ImageOps.py so we can split functions/sections.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: We both must touch exif_transpose in the same two files - true disjoint split isn't possible. Propose serializing instead: I'll add my strip_metadata param first (small, isolated addition at end of signature, minimal diff to existing lines), send DONE with exact diff summary, then you layer transform_callback on top. I'll avoid touching docstring lines you might also touch beyond adding my own new lines. Ok with this order? If you'd rather go first, say so and I'll wait.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: We both touch exif_transpose in the same files, so file-level disjointness isn't possible. To avoid clobbering: I'll create an isolated git worktree (separate branch) to implement transform_callback, diff against original HEAD, and produce patch.txt there without touching the shared working tree. Suggest you do the same for strip_metadata (your own worktree/branch). That way neither of us stomps on the other's concurrent edits in the shared tree. Proceeding this way unless you object - ACCEPT to confirm.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Accepted. I'll create my own worktree/branch, implement strip_metadata there, generate my diff, then write it to /workspace/repo/patch.txt without touching the shared working tree. Will send DONE when finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed conceptually, but to fully de-risk clobbering I'll just work in my own isolated git worktree off current HEAD (5d52ede58) rather than the shared tree - so I don't need to wait on your edits at all, and your working tree stays untouched by me. I'll produce patch.txt from that worktree. Go ahead with your approach independently; no blocking dependency between us. Will send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished transform_callback feature in isolated worktree /tmp/agent2-work (never touched the shared /workspace/repo tree). Added optional transform_callback param to exif_transpose (all 3 overload signatures) that, when given, is called with the orientation int and returns None/single Transpose/sequence of Transpose to override the default mapping; falls back to original mapping when not provided. Added 4 new tests. All 38 tests in test_imageops.py pass. patch.txt written to /workspace/repo/patch.txt containing only my diff. Your shared tree should be untouched by me - go ahead and finalize your own patch.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished strip_metadata param for exif_transpose. Implemented in an isolated worktree (now removed), diff written only to /workspace/repo/patch.txt - the shared working tree in /workspace/repo was never modified, so it's clean for you to build on. Summary of my exif_transpose changes for your awareness when you layer transform_callback on top: added a new keyword-only param 'strip_metadata: bool = False' (added last in both @overload signatures and the impl signature), added one docstring :param: line, and 3 new code blocks: (1) after the existing orientation-tag-removal block inside 'if method is not None:', added 'if strip_metadata: exif_image.info.clear(); for key in list(exif.keys()): del exif[key]'; (2) in 'elif not in_place:' branch changed 'return image.copy()' to assign image = image.copy() then conditionally clear info before returning; (3) added new 'elif strip_metadata:' branch before the final 'return None' to clear info/exif when in_place and no transpose occurred. No other lines touched. Good luck - ping if you want the exact diff pasted.
